metrics.narrative: Heinrich Zaguirre described real, specific manual pain on the 8 Jan 2026 call: vendor onboarding via emailed PDF forms stored in SharePoint (~100 active vendors), manual invoice review from a shared AP inbox, and manual payment-file uploads to the relevant stakeholder 8 May 2026 demo reinforced this: invoices enter via a shared Outlook mailbox, are manually downloaded to SharePoint, validated against contracts, then coded into QuickBooks Online. No buyer-voiced dollar or time-savings figure exists; Zycus supplied estimated commercial figures ($80K-150K annually, 8 Jan 2026) but the buyer has not echoed a quantified business case.

champion.narrative: Heinrich Zaguirre (Senior Manager, IT) has run point since the very first call (8 Jan 2026), scoped requirements, coordinated the 8 May 2026 demo, and pushed Zycus to investigate a Canada-region payment partner. That is coordination, not confirmed internal advocacy — no quote shows him selling Zycus internally. He was reported on vacation as of 19 Jun 2026, coinciding with the deal's current stall, which puts single-thread champion risk front and center.

sources: Avoma demo 2026-05-08 (competitor note re: Michael McCarthy)
competition.narrative: Salesforce's Competitors__c field lists eight names (Coupa, GEP Worldwide, iValua, JAGGAER, Oracle, SAP Ariba, Workday, Ironclad), but the buyer explicitly declined to name any competitor when asked directly on 8 Jan 2026 ('At this moment in time, no... I wanna make sure that you guys are not tailoring it such that you're poking at the bad points of them'), confirming only that some options are Gartner Magic Quadrant-listed and some are smaller-scale. Zycus's own rep (Michael McCarthy) has direct Coupa and Oracle tenure used for positioning, but that is our side's framing, not buyer preference.

identify_pain.narrative: Pain is well and specifically articulated: manual PDF-based vendor onboarding stored in SharePoint, no self-service vendor portal, manual line-by-line invoice review from a shared inbox, manual GL coding, and manual payment-file creation/upload to BMO for cross-border and domestic payments. Both the 8 Jan 2026 intro call and 8 May 2026 demo reconfirmed the same pain points independently, which is a strong signal of genuine, persistent need.

sources: Absence across Next_Step__c, Next_Step_History__c, and both read Avoma calls
paper_process.narrative: No MSA, SOW, or contracting artifact has surfaced anywhere in the record. This is expected at Formal Evaluation, but nothing (redlines, legal contact, procurement onboarding) has even been mentioned as upcoming.

sources: Salesforce Account contact roles (Jennifer Newman, COO)
economic_buyer.narrative: Heinrich Zaguirre named the final approver only by role on 8 Jan 2026 ('it goes to... our COO... can you approve this payment amount?'), and no COO has joined a call or been introduced in the six months since. Jennifer Newman is Salesforce's account-level COO contact, but no call or email ties her by name to this specific deal, so the link is unconfirmed. Anthony Fonzo (Director, Finance & Operations) and Denni Wong (Director, IT) are named as the working decision-makers per the 8 May 2026 demo notes, but neither controls final budget sign-off per Heinrich's own description of the approval workflow.

decision_process.narrative: The internal approval flow was described on 8 Jan 2026: a business-unit owner signs off, then the COO approves the payment amount. The 8 May 2026 demo drew both IT (Heinrich Zaguirre, Denni Wong, Shifa Garewal) and Finance/Ops (Anthony Fonzo, Jasmine Shen) attendees, confirming a joint IT+Finance evaluation. No formal timeline or gate sequence beyond 'the next 4 months' (Heinrich, 8 Jan 2026) and a loose dependency on the MFAM office move (~June 2026) has been documented.

decision_criteria.narrative: Detailed criteria surfaced across both calls: a centralized/self-service vendor onboarding portal to replace PDF/SharePoint, OCR-driven invoice capture, configurable approval workflows, native Microsoft Teams interaction (MFAM is a 'Teamshop'), payment-file generation or remittance capability tied to TransferMate, and supplier-risk scoring. The buyer explicitly said cost is a factor but not the primary driver ('I wouldn't wanna say cost is an issue... but obviously cost will play a role,' Heinrich Zaguirre, 8 Jan 2026).

items.name: Bailey's follow-up nudge on stalled decision
items.summary: Bailey Erazo re-surfaces the thread after an apparently cut-off prior email, asking MFAM directly if they need anything else to make a decision — a clear signal the deal has gone quiet since the proposal and integration Q&A were sent mid-June.

items.name: Prior context: proposal and integration answer (referenced in thread)
items.summary: The chained history shows Bailey sent the Transfermate/Zycus proposal on 6/17 and, on 6/9, answered Jasmine's question confirming Zycus can integrate with any payment platform if MFAM skips Transfermate, with pricing unchanged — MFAM has not responded to either.

day_summary.overall: After weeks of silence since a mid-June proposal exchange, Bailey (Zycus AE) followed up again on 6/30 chasing MFAM for a decision, essentially re-sending unanswered questions about integration and payment platform flexibility. The ball is firmly in MFAM's court now — Zycus has already answered the Transfermate/third-party payment integration question and confirmed pricing holds regardless of payment platform choice, but no reply has come back from Anthony, Jasmine, or Chris.

cro_panel.intro: The deal shows genuine, well-documented pain and a real, well-attended demo, but momentum has stalled for roughly six weeks behind an unresolved TransferMate pricing delay, no economic-buyer engagement exists after six months, and the competitive field is undefined.; The stated close date is not achievable given the outstanding implementation runway

flags: No Economic Buyer (COO or CFO) has been identified by name or engaged, and without EB sponsorship this $100k annual SaaS decision cannot advance to budget approval or contract. Heinrich stated on 2026-01-08 that 'our COO' is the final approver for payment amounts, clearly identifying the COO role as budget authority. However, no COO has joined any call or been introduced, and the demo on 2026-05-08 was attended only by mid-level IT and Finance stakeholders (Heinrich, Anthony DiFonzo, Denni Wong, Shifa Garewal, Jasmine Shen). The immediate move is to surface the COO or CFO through Anthony DiFonzo or Denni Wong, position this as a strategic finance-automation and cash-flow initiative (not an IT project), and secure a 20-minute executive briefing within 14 days. Frame it as: 'We've validated technical and operational fit with your IT and AP teams; the next step is to align the business case and ROI model with your COO/CFO so we can move to commercial discussion.' Without EB engagement this deal will stall indefinitely at mid-level evaluation.

MFAM is evaluating Gartner Magic Quadrant leaders (Coupa, GEP, SAP Ariba, Oracle) and smaller-scale vendors, but Zycus has no visibility into competitive ranking, who else has demoed, what MFAM liked/disliked about competitors, or whether Zycus is on a shortlist. The deal is 170 days old with no down-select or finalist status communicated. The immediate move is to extract competitive intelligence from Heinrich (now on vacation, so pivot to Anthony DiFonzo or Denni Wong): ask directly 'Who else have you seen demo? What did you like about their approach? Where do we rank?' Then reposition with the Merlin AI wedge—Zycus's agentic automation, Microsoft Teams integration, and modern UX are differentiated vs Coupa/Ariba's legacy platforms, and the owner's manager Michael McCarthy (who spent 4 years at Coupa) can position against Coupa's complexity and cost. Make Merlin the centerpiece of the finalist pitch: 'The Gartner platforms you're evaluating were built 15-20 years ago and bolted AI on recently; Zycus Merlin is AI-native and embeds agentic automation into every workflow—supplier onboarding, invoice OCR, duplicate detection, and payment execution—so your 33-person AP and IT teams spend zero time on manual data entry.' This is the win strategy for a crowded Gartner-led evaluation.
Lead the next AP-team touchpoint with the Merlin/Teams chatbot - Heinrich called it something 'no other company does'; it is the clearest differentiator against an undisclosed Gartner-MQ field.
